- `GET /workspaces/:id/manifest` — Get manifest + tasks
- `POST /workspaces/:id/retry-setup` — Retry failed setup

**Query hooks:**
- `useWorkspacesByRepo()` — WS subscription + HTTP fallback, staleTime: Infinity
- `useStats()` — WS subscription + HTTP fallback, staleTime: Infinity

- `useManifestTasks()` — staleTime: Infinity

- `useArchiveWorkspace()` — Optimistic removal, invalidates on settle
- `useUnarchiveWorkspace()` — Sets state to "ready", invalidates on settle
- `useUpdateSystemPrompt()` — Optimistic update
- `useRetrySetup()` — Invalidates all workspaces on settle

content.thinking:
Interesting — `pr_url` is never written to the database! It's a column in the schema, it's read in queries, and `gh.service.ts` returns `pr_url` from GitHub API calls. But there's no `UPDATE workspaces SET pr_url = ...` anywhere in the codebase.

This means `pr_url` is populated at read time (from the GitHub API) rather than persisted. The `usePRStatus()` hook calls the gh service which checks GitHub directly.

So the auto-derive trigger for "in-review" can't be "when pr_url is written to DB" because it's never written. Instead, the trigger needs to be different:


The frontend already polls PR status every 30-60 seconds via `usePRStatus()`, so that's where the status change could be triggered. Or the backend could check PR status when certain events happen. Though looking closer, `pr_url` and `pr_number` might be leftover columns from an older design since I can't find where they're actually populated.

For the auto-derive trigger, the cleanest approach would be to add a side-effect in the PR status check itself—when `getPRStatus()` detects a new PR or a merged PR, it could automatically update the workspace status accordingly, rather than putting that logic in the frontend. content.content:
Add workspace event emission system with Redis persistence|## Summary
Implement comprehensive workspace event system with Redis-backed persistence for real-time WebSocket streaming and HTTP history fetching. Adds event emission on workspace lifecycle, file operations, git operations, and command execution. Events are fire-and-forget and never block workspace operations.

## Changes
- **New WorkspaceEventEmitter**: Redis stream management with XADD/XREVRANGE/XREAD
- **Event emission**: File writes, deletes, command start/finish, git operations
- **HTTP endpoint**: GET /workspaces/{id}/events for fetching recent events
- **Async context manager pattern**: Modern Python async resource management
- **Comprehensive tests**: 22 unit & integration tests (4 with Redis)

🤖 Generated with Claude Code

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * HTTP endpoint to fetch recent workspace events with pagination
  * Workspace actions (file write/delete, commands, git ops, lifecycle) now emit observable events
  * Optional Redis-backed event streaming with a local no-op fallback

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Improved HTTP error handling and clearer failures when event history is unavailable

* **Tests**
  * Comprehensive tests for event types, emitters, retrieval, lifecycle, and end-to-end flows

<sub>✏️ Tip: You can customize this high-level summary in your review settings.</sub>
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->|in-review|ready
Fix sandbox race condition in project creation|## Summary

- Prevent multiple sandboxes from being created when initializing a project
- When sandbox is in "initializing" state, subsequent calls wait instead of creating duplicates
- Added `cloneCompleted` field to allow chat messages to start as soon as repository is cloned

## Testing

E2E test verifies only one sandbox is created per project and `/sandbox/start` correctly returns `reused: true`.

feat: integrate code-server (VS Code in browser) into E2B sandbox|## Summary
Adds VS Code in-browser editor (`code-server`) to the Expo Agent's E2B sandbox environment. Users can now edit code directly in the preview panel via a new Editor tab.

## Changes
- Installs code-server in E2B template via official installer
- Creates systemd service (`echo-code-server.service`) running on port 18080
- Updates template initialization and service startup scripts
- Adds Editor tab to preview panel with iframe embedding code-server
- Updates E2B template IDs after successful build

## Test Plan
- ✓ Built E2B templates with code-server included
- ✓ Verified systemd service starts and listens on port 18080
- ✓ Populated backend/.dev.vars with E2B_API_KEY for sandbox creation
- ✓ Frontend Editor tab correctly embeds code-server iframe
- Ready to test end-to-end project creation with editor access

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)|in-review|ready
feat: add visual element inspector with chat integration|## Visual Inspector for Expo Agent Preview

Adds a floating element inspector panel to the Expo Agent web preview, enabling users to:
- Click elements in the preview to inspect their properties (position, styles, classes, text content)
- Edit styles, text, and classes in real-time
- Send selected element information to the chat for AI refinement

## Implementation

**Frontend**: InspectorPanel component + useVisualEditBridge hook for postMessage communication with the preview iframe. "Send to Chat" button injects element data into chat input.

**Backend**: Packages expo-visual-edit as a tarball and injects it at the Metro bundler level via custom Babel transformer + setup module, without modifying user source files.

## Security

Added `event.source` validation on incoming postMessage to prevent spoofing from other iframes.

🤖 Generated with Claude Code|in-review|ready

# Check if OpenDevs has a pr_url column - it doesn't seem to, but let's check how derived_status gets set to in-review
# Also check what our codebase does with pr_url - it's in schema but never written
sqlite3 "$HOME/Library/Application Support/com.conductor.app/conductor.db" "
SELECT COUNT(*) as total,
  SUM(CASE WHEN pr_title IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as has_pr,
  SUM(CASE WHEN derived_status = 'in-review' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as in_review
FROM workspaces;" 2>&1
input.description: Check PR vs in-review correlation in OpenDevs

input.command:
# So 218 have pr_title but only 14 are in-review. Let's understand better
sqlite3 "$HOME/Library/Application Support/com.conductor.app/conductor.db" "
SELECT derived_status, state,
  SUM(CASE WHEN pr_title IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as has_pr,
  COUNT(*) as total
FROM workspaces
GROUP BY derived_status, state
ORDER BY total DESC;" 2>&1

content.text:
Interesting findings:

**OpenDevs stores `pr_title` and `pr_description` but NOT `pr_url`.** It has 218 workspaces with PR titles, but only 14 are `in-review` — meaning `derived_status = 'in-review'` is set when a PR is created (active), and archived PRs revert to `done` or stay `in-progress`.

OpenDevs stores PR metadata for the **PR creation flow** — when you create a PR from the workspace, it saves the title/description you drafted. It doesn't store the URL because the PR status is fetched live from GitHub (same as us).

Now to your question about "what if we want different PR reviews" — do you mean:

1. **Multiple PRs per workspace** — e.g., a workspace that creates a PR, gets it closed, creates another?
2. **Different review tools** — e.g., not just GitHub PRs, but also GitLab MRs, or internal review flows?
3. **PR drafts** — storing the title/description the user drafts before actually creating the PR?

The user asked about storing pr_url and whether we should, and what about different PR reviews. I presented some findings and asked clarifying questions. But I'm in plan mode and need to use AskUserQuestion for clarifications. Let me search for it properly.

Actually, looking at the tool search results, it seems AskUserQuestion wasn't found as a deferred tool. Let me try again.

content.content: No matching deferred tools found

content.text:
Here's what I found about OpenDevs and PR storage:

**OpenDevs stores `pr_title` and `pr_description` but NOT `pr_url`.** It persists the PR draft metadata (what you typed when creating the PR), not the live GitHub state. PR status is fetched live from GitHub, same as us.

The data tells an interesting story: 218 workspaces have `pr_title` but only 14 are `in-review` — because most PRs were already merged/archived by the time the status feature shipped.

---

On your question about storing `pr_url` and different PR reviews — I want to clarify what you're thinking:

1. **Store `pr_url` for auto-derive** — We already have the column in the schema, it's just never written. We could persist it when PR status is fetched, giving us a reliable signal for auto-deriving `in-review`. This is clean.

2. **"Different PR reviews"** — Could you clarify what you mean? Are you thinking about:
   - **Multiple PRs per workspace** (close one, open another)?
   - **Non-GitHub review flows** (GitLab, Bitbucket, manual code review)?
   - **Storing PR draft content** like OpenDevs does (`pr_title`, `pr_description`) for the PR creation UI?

This matters for the schema design — if we want to support multiple PRs or different review tools, a simple `pr_url` column on the workspace table might not be enough. We might want a separate `reviews` table or a more flexible model.

What's your thinking here?

Excellent! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the codebase. Let me create a detailed summary document based on my thorough exploration:

## Comprehensive Architecture Analysis

Based on my thorough exploration of the codebase, here's the current state of the application after the major refactor:

### **1. Backend Agent Service Layer**

**Location**: `backend/src/services/agent/`

The agent service consists of several focused modules:

- **client.ts** (16KB): WebSocket client connecting to the sidecar (agent-server) using JSON-RPC 2.0 protocol
  - Handles initialize/initialized handshake
  - Implements auto-reconnect with exponential backoff (max 30s)
  - Registers handlers for all canonical agent events and frontend-facing RPC methods
  - Uses `json-rpc-2.0` library for bidirectional communication

- **service.ts** (3.2KB): Composition root and dependency injection
  - Singleton instance managing the AgentClient lifecycle
  - Injects event handler with `respondToAgent` dependency to break circularity
  - Exposes typed wrappers: `forwardTurn()`, `respondToAgent()`, `stopSession()`, `isConnected()`
  - Called once at startup via `server.ts`

- **commands.ts** (5.6KB): Business logic for `q:command` dispatch
  - `handleSendMessage()`: Persists user message, forwards to agent-server, builds turn options
  - `handleStopSession()`: Updates session status to idle, notifies agent-server
  - Validates params, performs DB writes, triggers invalidation
  - Recovers `agent_session_id` from DB for session resume

- **event-handler.ts** (6.8KB): Single entry point for all agent → backend data flow
  - Factory `createAgentEventHandler()` with injected `respondToAgent` dependency
  - Dispatches all 12 canonical event types using `ts-pattern` match
  - Calls `persistXxx()` then `invalidate()` for each event type
  - Handles tool relay coordination: `relayToolRequest()` awaits frontend response, then calls `respondToAgent()`

- **persistence.ts** (9.1KB): Database write functions for canonical agent events
  - `persistAssistantMessage()`, `persistToolResultMessage()`, `persistMessageCancelled()`
  - `persistSessionStarted()`, `persistSessionIdle()`, `persistSessionError()`, `persistSessionCancelled()`
  - `persistAgentSessionId()`: Stores agent-provided session ID for resume
  - All functions synchronous (better-sqlite3), return `WriteResult<T>`
  - Messages auto-get seq from trigger, sessions track denormalized counts

- **tool-relay.ts** (4.2KB): Manages bidirectional tool requests from agent to frontend
  - `relay()`: Broadcasts q:event to all WS clients, returns promise resolving when frontend responds
  - `resolve()/reject()`: Called by query-engine when q:tool_response arrives
  - Tracks pending relays by requestId with configurable timeouts
  - Used by event-handler's `relayToolRequest()` to await frontend result, then respond to agent

- **index.ts** (560B): Barrel export of public API

**Data Flow**:
```
Agent SDK → AgentHandler → AgentServer → Client (JSON-RPC)
  ↓
Backend AgentClient receives canonical AgentEvent
  ↓
agent-event-handler dispatches (match on type)
  ↓
persistence writes to DB (asserting write success)
  ↓
invalidate() pushes q:snapshot/q:delta to WS subscribers
```

---

### **2. Sidecar Changes (Agent-Server)**

**Location**: `sidecar/` — Now stateless JSON-RPC 2.0 server, no DB access

**index.ts** (653 lines):
- Unified transport: WebSocket (default, dynamic port) or Unix socket (legacy)
- Graceful shutdown: drain in-flight turns (30s timeout), close HTTP listener, cancel remaining sessions
- Registers agents (Claude, Codex) and initializes on startup
- `setupJsonRpc()` wires all RPC methods:
  - `initialize`: Returns agents and capabilities
  - `turn/start`: New wire protocol, maps to agent.query(), tracks session for shutdown drain
  - `turn/cancel`, `session/stop`, `session/reset`: Dispatch to all agents
  - `provider/auth`, `provider/initWorkspace`, `provider/contextUsage`, `provider/updateMode`: Delegate to agent
  - `agent/list`: Introspection endpoint
- Logs buffered to `/tmp/opendevs-{pid}.log` with configurable LOG_LEVEL (debug/info/error)
- Outputs `LISTEN_URL=ws://127.0.0.1:{port}` to stdout for Rust process manager to parse

**event-broadcaster.ts** (619 lines):
- Singleton that manages multi-tunnel broadcasting (backend + relay can be connected simultaneously)
- **Notifications** (sidecar → frontend):
  - `sendMessage()`, `sendError()`: Legacy format (during dual-write)
  - `emitEvent()`: Broadcasts canonical AgentEvent as JSON-RPC notification
- **Canonical event emission** (dual-write period):
  - `emitSessionStarted()`, `emitSessionIdle()`, `emitSessionError()`, `emitSessionCancelled()`
  - `emitAssistantMessage()`, `emitToolResultMessage()`, `emitMessageCancelled()`, `emitMessageResult()`
  - `emitRequestOpened()`, `emitToolRequest()`
  - `emitAgentSessionId()`
- **Frontend-facing RPC requests** with timeouts:
  - Browser: `snapshot`, `click`, `type`, `navigate`, `getState`, `waitFor`, `evaluate`, `pressKey`, `hover`, `selectOption`, `navigateBack`, `consoleMessages`, `networkRequests`, `screenshot`, `scroll`
  - Simulator: `screenshot`, `tap`, `swipe`, `typeText`, `pressKey`, `buildAndRun`, `listDevices`, `start`
  - Workspace: `getDiff`, `diffComment`, `getTerminalOutput`
  - User interaction: `askUserQuestion`, `exitPlanMode`
- Timeouts: User-facing 120s, browser snapshot 20s, navigate 30s, simulator boot 30s, build 10m

**health.ts**, **rpc-connection.ts**, **agents/registry.ts**, **agents/session-store.ts**, **agents/lifecycle.ts**:
- Health endpoints (`/health`, `/readyz`)
- JSON-RPC bidirectional peer using `json-rpc-2.0`
- Agent registration and initialization
- Session tracking for graceful shutdown drain
- Error classification (internal/network/user_input/invalid_request)

---

### **3. Shared Protocol & Event Types**

**shared/agent-events.ts** (401 lines):
- **12 canonical event types** (discriminated union):
  - Session: `session.started`, `session.idle`, `session.error`, `session.cancelled`
  - Messages: `message.assistant`, `message.tool_result`, `message.result`, `message.cancelled`
  - Interactions: `request.opened`, `request.resolved`
  - Tool: `tool.request`
  - Metadata: `agent.session_id`
- Each event is Zod-validated, contains sessionId (and agentType for non-metadata)
- RPC method constants: `AGENT_RPC_METHODS`, `FRONTEND_RPC_METHODS`
- Capabilities schema (auth, workspaceInit, contextUsage, modelSwitch, multiTurn, sessionResume, permissionMode)
- Turn options: cwd, model, maxThinkingTokens, maxTurns, turnId, permissionMode, resume, etc.

**shared/types/query-protocol.ts** (wire format):
- Client frames: `q:subscribe`, `q:unsubscribe`, `q:request`, `q:mutate`, `q:command`, `q:tool_response`
- Server frames: `q:snapshot`, `q:delta`, `q:response`, `q:mutate_result`, `q:command_ack`, `q:event`, `q:error`
- Subscription-based push with ID-keyed routing
- Cursor-based deltas for messages (messages use `q:delta`, others use `q:snapshot`)

**shared/types/workspace.ts**:
- Workspace entity includes: `pr_url`, `pr_number` (nullable) — retrieved from GitHub via `gh pr list`
- Session includes: `agent_session_id` (for resume), `status` (idle/working/error), `error_category`

**shared/schema.ts** (database schema, idempotent):
- 5 tables: repositories, workspaces, sessions, messages, paired_devices
- 10 indexes: workspace by repo/state, session by workspace/status, messages by seq/sent_at/session_role/turn_id/parent_tool_use
- 5 triggers: 3 auto-update `updated_at`, 2 manage message seq auto-increment and session message_count denormalization
- Workspaces: `pr_url`, `pr_number`, `archive_commit`, `archived_at`, `setup_status`, `init_stage`
- Sessions: `agent_session_id`, `error_category`, `context_token_count`, `context_used_percent`, `last_user_message_at`
- Messages: `seq` (per-session monotonic integer), `parent_tool_use_id` (for tool result linking)

---

### **4. Backend Query Engine & Route Layer**

**backend/src/services/query-engine.ts** (handles q:* frames):
- Subscription routing: per-connection active subs keyed by client-assigned ID
- `handleFrame()` dispatches q:subscribe/q:unsubscribe/q:request/q:mutate/q:command/q:tool_response
- `runQuery()` executes typed queries: workspaces, stats, sessions, messages
- `invalidate()` pushes fresh data to active subscribers
  - Messages use `q:delta` (cursor-based, only new messages since last seq)
  - Workspaces can use targeted `q:delta` when sessionIds provided (only changed workspaces)
  - Fallback to `q:snapshot` if delta lookup fails
- Tool relay: q:tool_response calls `resolveToolRelay()`/`rejectToolRelay()` in tool-relay.ts

**backend/src/routes/workspaces.ts**:
- GET /workspaces (by-repo, paginated), GET /workspaces/:id, PATCH /workspaces/:id (title, branch)
- Workspace creation coordinates: Rust git ops + DB + session initialization
- Queries use views/joins to include repo + active session data

**backend/src/routes/workspaces.pr.ts**:
- GET /gh-status: Check if gh CLI installed and authenticated (cached on frontend)
- GET /workspaces/:id/pr-status: Returns PrStatusResponse (async, fork-aware, explicit error classification)
  - Detects fork (upstream vs origin)
  - Calls `gh pr list --head <branch> --author @me --state all`
  - Classifies CI status (passing/failing/pending) from statusCheckRollup
  - Maps review decision (approved/changes_requested/review_required)
  - Returns pr_number, pr_url, pr_state (open/merged/closed), merge_status (ready/blocked/merged), is_draft, has_conflicts

**backend/src/services/gh.service.ts**:
- `runGh()`: Executes gh CLI with timeouts (5s default), classifies errors
- `getPrStatus()`: Resolves HEAD branch, queries GitHub for PRs, handles forks
- Persistent state: Workspace.pr_url, Workspace.pr_number (queried on-demand)

**backend/src/db/queries.ts** & **backend/src/db/types.ts**:
- Workspace rows include: pr_url, pr_number
- Queries use better-sqlite3 with preparedStatement.all() / .get()
- Message pagination: index on (session_id, seq DESC), limit + offset

---

### **5. Frontend WebSocket & Query Subscription**

**src/platform/ws/query-protocol-client.ts** (singleton):
- Opens WebSocket to backend /ws endpoint
- Auto-reconnect with exponential backoff (500ms → 8s max)
- `subscribe()`: Register onSnapshot/onDelta callbacks, returns unsubscribe function
- `sendCommand()`: Send q:command, wait for q:command_ack (with 30s timeout)
- `onEvent()`: Register callback for q:event broadcasts (e.g., tool:request)
- Re-establishes all subscriptions on reconnect
- Handles ping/pong keep-alive

**src/shared/hooks/useWsToolRequest.ts**:
- Hook that listens for q:event "tool:request" and dispatches to handler callback
- Handler decides which methods to claim (match on method name)
- Sends response via q:tool_response (requestId + result or error)
- Module-level Set deduplicates responses across multiple hook instances
- Used by browser, simulator, and workspace RPC handlers

**src/shared/api/queryKeys.ts**:
- React Query key factory for workspaces, sessions, messages, stats
- Used by useQuerySubscription hook to auto-cache results

**src/shared/hooks/useQuerySubscription.ts** (custom React Query hook):
- Subscribes to WS resource, pushes snapshots into React Query cache
- Auto-unsubscribe on unmount
- Merges deltas client-side using `mergeDelta` function

**Data Flow (Frontend)**:
```
Frontend → q:subscribe/q:command → Backend WS
Backend → q:snapshot/q:delta/q:command_ack/q:event → Frontend
Frontend tool handler → q:tool_response → Backend
Backend tool-relay → respondToAgent → Agent-Server
```

---

### **6. PR Tracking & Workspace State**

**PR-related data**:
- Stored in `workspaces` table: `pr_url TEXT`, `pr_number INTEGER` (nullable)
- Queried on-demand via `GET /workspaces/:id/pr-status`
- Not persisted directly; computed each time via `gh pr list --head <branch>`
- Workspace displays PR state indicator (open/merged/closed) in sidebar
- CI status (passing/failing/pending) shown from statusCheckRollup
- Merge status (ready/blocked) from mergeable field
- Fork detection: upstream vs origin remote comparison

**Workspace state transitions**:
- initializing → ready (via workspace creation)
- ready → archived (via archive action)
- Sessions track: idle/working/error (persisted in sessions.status)

---

### **7. Current Architecture Summary**

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Frontend (React + Zustand + React Query)                        │
│ - useQuerySubscription: WS push → React Query cache             │
│ - useWsToolRequest: Listen for tool:request → respond           │
│ - WorkspaceItem: Displays pr_url, pr_number, session_status     │
└────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────┘
                             │ WebSocket q:* protocol
                             ↓
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Backend (Hono + Node.js)                                        │
│ - query-engine.ts: Routes q:subscribe/q:command                 │
│ - agent/service.ts: Manages AgentClient → sidecar               │
│ - agent/event-handler.ts: Canonical events → DB + invalidate    │
│ - agent/persistence.ts: DB writes for events                    │
│ - agent/tool-relay.ts: Tool request ↔ frontend                  │
│ - gh.service.ts: Queries GitHub via gh CLI                      │
│ - Database: SQLite (repositories, workspaces, sessions, msgs)   │
└────────────┬──────────────────────────────────┬──────────────────┘
             │ JSON-RPC 2.0                     │ REST
             ↓                                  ↓
   ┌─────────────────────┐          ┌──────────────────┐
   │ Agent-Server        │          │ Tauri IPC        │
   │ (sidecar, stateless)│          │ Git operations   │
   │ - Claude/Codex SDK  │          │ File scanning    │
   │ - EventBroadcaster  │          │ Terminal I/O     │
   │ - Health endpoints  │          └──────────────────┘
   └────────┬────────────┘
            ↓
   ┌─────────────────────┐
   │ Agent SDKs          │
   │ - Claude API        │
   │ - Tool execution    │
   └─────────────────────┘
```

---

### **8. Key Files & Their Purposes**

| File | Lines | Purpose |
|------|-------|---------|
| `backend/src/services/agent/client.ts` | 454 | JSON-RPC 2.0 WebSocket client → agent-server |
| `backend/src/services/agent/event-handler.ts` | 162 | Dispatch canonical events → persistence + invalidate |
| `backend/src/services/agent/persistence.ts` | 248 | DB writes for all event types |
| `backend/src/services/agent/tool-relay.ts` | 132 | Bidirectional tool request relay |
| `backend/src/services/query-engine.ts` | ~400 | q:* protocol frame dispatch |
| `sidecar/index.ts` | 653 | Agent-server entry point, transport agnostic |
| `sidecar/event-broadcaster.ts` | 619 | Multi-tunnel notification broadcaster |
| `shared/agent-events.ts` | 401 | Canonical event type definitions |
| `shared/schema.ts` | 143 | Database schema (5 tables, 10 indexes) |
| `src/platform/ws/query-protocol-client.ts` | ~400 | Frontend WS client singleton |
| `src/shared/hooks/useWsToolRequest.ts` | 100 | Frontend tool request listener hook |

---

### **9. Critical Data Flows**

**User sends message**:
1. Frontend: q:command { command: "sendMessage", sessionId, content, model }
2. Backend commands.ts: handleSendMessage() → writeUserMessage() → invalidate() → agent.forwardTurn()
3. Agent-server: agent.query() spawns Claude SDK stream
4. Agent-server: Emits canonical events (message.assistant, session.idle, etc.)
5. Backend event-handler: Listens on AgentClient, persists to DB, calls invalidate()
6. Query-engine: Pushes q:delta/q:snapshot to WS subscribers

**Tool request (browser, simulator, diff)**:
1. Agent-server calls EventBroadcaster.requestBrowserXxx()
2. Sidecar emits tool.request event via JSON-RPC to backend
3. Backend event-handler calls relay() → broadcasts q:event "tool:request"
4. Frontend useWsToolRequest hook catches event, dispatches to handler
5. Handler responds via q:tool_response
6. Backend tool-relay.resolve() resolves pending promise
7. event-handler sends respondToAgent() back to agent-server

**PR status query**:
1. Frontend requests GET /workspaces/:id/pr-status
2. Backend gh.service resolves HEAD branch, queries gh CLI
3. Returns pr_number, pr_url, pr_state, ci_status, review_status, merge_status
4. Workspace sidebar displays PR indicator

---

### **10. No Direct DB Access in Sidecar**

The agent-server is completely stateless:
- No database connection
- No persistence layer
- Events stream to backend via JSON-RPC notifications
- Backend owns all DB writes and invalidation logic
- Sidecar can be restarted without data loss

This design keeps the sidecar lightweight and fast, allowing rapid agent SDK upgrades without schema migrations.

---

This completes the comprehensive architecture analysis. The system is now fully decoupled with clear ownership: Backend handles persistence & subscriptions, Sidecar handles agent SDKs & tools, Frontend handles UI & WS protocol.

content.text:
Excellent! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the codebase. Let me create a detailed summary document based on my thorough exploration:

## Comprehensive Architecture Analysis

Based on my thorough exploration of the codebase, here's the current state of the application after the major refactor:

### **1. Backend Agent Service Layer**

**Location**: `backend/src/services/agent/`

The agent service consists of several focused modules:

- **client.ts** (16KB): WebSocket client connecting to the sidecar (agent-server) using JSON-RPC 2.0 protocol
  - Handles initialize/initialized handshake
  - Implements auto-reconnect with exponential backoff (max 30s)
  - Registers handlers for all canonical agent events and frontend-facing RPC methods
  - Uses `json-rpc-2.0` library for bidirectional communication

- **service.ts** (3.2KB): Composition root and dependency injection
  - Singleton instance managing the AgentClient lifecycle
  - Injects event handler with `respondToAgent` dependency to break circularity
  - Exposes typed wrappers: `forwardTurn()`, `respondToAgent()`, `stopSession()`, `isConnected()`
  - Called once at startup via `server.ts`

- **commands.ts** (5.6KB): Business logic for `q:command` dispatch
  - `handleSendMessage()`: Persists user message, forwards to agent-server, builds turn options
  - `handleStopSession()`: Updates session status to idle, notifies agent-server
  - Validates params, performs DB writes, triggers invalidation
  - Recovers `agent_session_id` from DB for session resume

- **event-handler.ts** (6.8KB): Single entry point for all agent → backend data flow
  - Factory `createAgentEventHandler()` with injected `respondToAgent` dependency
  - Dispatches all 12 canonical event types using `ts-pattern` match
  - Calls `persistXxx()` then `invalidate()` for each event type
  - Handles tool relay coordination: `relayToolRequest()` awaits frontend response, then calls `respondToAgent()`

- **persistence.ts** (9.1KB): Database write functions for canonical agent events
  - `persistAssistantMessage()`, `persistToolResultMessage()`, `persistMessageCancelled()`
  - `persistSessionStarted()`, `persistSessionIdle()`, `persistSessionError()`, `persistSessionCancelled()`
  - `persistAgentSessionId()`: Stores agent-provided session ID for resume
  - All functions synchronous (better-sqlite3), return `WriteResult<T>`
  - Messages auto-get seq from trigger, sessions track denormalized counts

- **tool-relay.ts** (4.2KB): Manages bidirectional tool requests from agent to frontend
  - `relay()`: Broadcasts q:event to all WS clients, returns promise resolving when frontend responds
  - `resolve()/reject()`: Called by query-engine when q:tool_response arrives
  - Tracks pending relays by requestId with configurable timeouts
  - Used by event-handler's `relayToolRequest()` to await frontend result, then respond to agent

- **index.ts** (560B): Barrel export of public API

**Data Flow**:
```
Agent SDK → AgentHandler → AgentServer → Client (JSON-RPC)
  ↓
Backend AgentClient receives canonical AgentEvent
  ↓
agent-event-handler dispatches (match on type)
  ↓
persistence writes to DB (asserting write success)
  ↓
invalidate() pushes q:snapshot/q:delta to WS subscribers
```

---

### **2. Sidecar Changes (Agent-Server)**

**Location**: `sidecar/` — Now stateless JSON-RPC 2.0 server, no DB access

**index.ts** (653 lines):
- Unified transport: WebSocket (default, dynamic port) or Unix socket (legacy)
- Graceful shutdown: drain in-flight turns (30s timeout), close HTTP listener, cancel remaining sessions
- Registers agents (Claude, Codex) and initializes on startup
- `setupJsonRpc()` wires all RPC methods:
  - `initialize`: Returns agents and capabilities
  - `turn/start`: New wire protocol, maps to agent.query(), tracks session for shutdown drain
  - `turn/cancel`, `session/stop`, `session/reset`: Dispatch to all agents
  - `provider/auth`, `provider/initWorkspace`, `provider/contextUsage`, `provider/updateMode`: Delegate to agent
  - `agent/list`: Introspection endpoint
- Logs buffered to `/tmp/opendevs-{pid}.log` with configurable LOG_LEVEL (debug/info/error)
- Outputs `LISTEN_URL=ws://127.0.0.1:{port}` to stdout for Rust process manager to parse

**event-broadcaster.ts** (619 lines):
- Singleton that manages multi-tunnel broadcasting (backend + relay can be connected simultaneously)
- **Notifications** (sidecar → frontend):
  - `sendMessage()`, `sendError()`: Legacy format (during dual-write)
  - `emitEvent()`: Broadcasts canonical AgentEvent as JSON-RPC notification
- **Canonical event emission** (dual-write period):
  - `emitSessionStarted()`, `emitSessionIdle()`, `emitSessionError()`, `emitSessionCancelled()`
  - `emitAssistantMessage()`, `emitToolResultMessage()`, `emitMessageCancelled()`, `emitMessageResult()`
  - `emitRequestOpened()`, `emitToolRequest()`
  - `emitAgentSessionId()`
- **Frontend-facing RPC requests** with timeouts:
  - Browser: `snapshot`, `click`, `type`, `navigate`, `getState`, `waitFor`, `evaluate`, `pressKey`, `hover`, `selectOption`, `navigateBack`, `consoleMessages`, `networkRequests`, `screenshot`, `scroll`
  - Simulator: `screenshot`, `tap`, `swipe`, `typeText`, `pressKey`, `buildAndRun`, `listDevices`, `start`
  - Workspace: `getDiff`, `diffComment`, `getTerminalOutput`
  - User interaction: `askUserQuestion`, `exitPlanMode`
- Timeouts: User-facing 120s, browser snapshot 20s, navigate 30s, simulator boot 30s, build 10m

**health.ts**, **rpc-connection.ts**, **agents/registry.ts**, **agents/session-store.ts**, **agents/lifecycle.ts**:
- Health endpoints (`/health`, `/readyz`)
- JSON-RPC bidirectional peer using `json-rpc-2.0`
- Agent registration and initialization
- Session tracking for graceful shutdown drain
- Error classification (internal/network/user_input/invalid_request)

---

### **3. Shared Protocol & Event Types**

**shared/agent-events.ts** (401 lines):
- **12 canonical event types** (discriminated union):
  - Session: `session.started`, `session.idle`, `session.error`, `session.cancelled`
  - Messages: `message.assistant`, `message.tool_result`, `message.result`, `message.cancelled`
  - Interactions: `request.opened`, `request.resolved`
  - Tool: `tool.request`
  - Metadata: `agent.session_id`
- Each event is Zod-validated, contains sessionId (and agentType for non-metadata)
- RPC method constants: `AGENT_RPC_METHODS`, `FRONTEND_RPC_METHODS`
- Capabilities schema (auth, workspaceInit, contextUsage, modelSwitch, multiTurn, sessionResume, permissionMode)
- Turn options: cwd, model, maxThinkingTokens, maxTurns, turnId, permissionMode, resume, etc.

**shared/types/query-protocol.ts** (wire format):
- Client frames: `q:subscribe`, `q:unsubscribe`, `q:request`, `q:mutate`, `q:command`, `q:tool_response`
- Server frames: `q:snapshot`, `q:delta`, `q:response`, `q:mutate_result`, `q:command_ack`, `q:event`, `q:error`
- Subscription-based push with ID-keyed routing
- Cursor-based deltas for messages (messages use `q:delta`, others use `q:snapshot`)

**shared/types/workspace.ts**:
- Workspace entity includes: `pr_url`, `pr_number` (nullable) — retrieved from GitHub via `gh pr list`
- Session includes: `agent_session_id` (for resume), `status` (idle/working/error), `error_category`

**shared/schema.ts** (database schema, idempotent):
- 5 tables: repositories, workspaces, sessions, messages, paired_devices
- 10 indexes: workspace by repo/state, session by workspace/status, messages by seq/sent_at/session_role/turn_id/parent_tool_use
- 5 triggers: 3 auto-update `updated_at`, 2 manage message seq auto-increment and session message_count denormalization
- Workspaces: `pr_url`, `pr_number`, `archive_commit`, `archived_at`, `setup_status`, `init_stage`
- Sessions: `agent_session_id`, `error_category`, `context_token_count`, `context_used_percent`, `last_user_message_at`
- Messages: `seq` (per-session monotonic integer), `parent_tool_use_id` (for tool result linking)

---

### **4. Backend Query Engine & Route Layer**

**backend/src/services/query-engine.ts** (handles q:* frames):
- Subscription routing: per-connection active subs keyed by client-assigned ID
- `handleFrame()` dispatches q:subscribe/q:unsubscribe/q:request/q:mutate/q:command/q:tool_response
- `runQuery()` executes typed queries: workspaces, stats, sessions, messages
- `invalidate()` pushes fresh data to active subscribers
  - Messages use `q:delta` (cursor-based, only new messages since last seq)
  - Workspaces can use targeted `q:delta` when sessionIds provided (only changed workspaces)
  - Fallback to `q:snapshot` if delta lookup fails
- Tool relay: q:tool_response calls `resolveToolRelay()`/`rejectToolRelay()` in tool-relay.ts

**backend/src/routes/workspaces.ts**:
- GET /workspaces (by-repo, paginated), GET /workspaces/:id, PATCH /workspaces/:id (title, branch)
- Workspace creation coordinates: Rust git ops + DB + session initialization
- Queries use views/joins to include repo + active session data

**backend/src/routes/workspaces.pr.ts**:
- GET /gh-status: Check if gh CLI installed and authenticated (cached on frontend)
- GET /workspaces/:id/pr-status: Returns PrStatusResponse (async, fork-aware, explicit error classification)
  - Detects fork (upstream vs origin)
  - Calls `gh pr list --head <branch> --author @me --state all`
  - Classifies CI status (passing/failing/pending) from statusCheckRollup
  - Maps review decision (approved/changes_requested/review_required)
  - Returns pr_number, pr_url, pr_state (open/merged/closed), merge_status (ready/blocked/merged), is_draft, has_conflicts

**backend/src/services/gh.service.ts**:
- `runGh()`: Executes gh CLI with timeouts (5s default), classifies errors
- `getPrStatus()`: Resolves HEAD branch, queries GitHub for PRs, handles forks
- Persistent state: Workspace.pr_url, Workspace.pr_number (queried on-demand)

**backend/src/db/queries.ts** & **backend/src/db/types.ts**:
- Workspace rows include: pr_url, pr_number
- Queries use better-sqlite3 with preparedStatement.all() / .get()
- Message pagination: index on (session_id, seq DESC), limit + offset

---

### **5. Frontend WebSocket & Query Subscription**

**src/platform/ws/query-protocol-client.ts** (singleton):
- Opens WebSocket to backend /ws endpoint
- Auto-reconnect with exponential backoff (500ms → 8s max)
- `subscribe()`: Register onSnapshot/onDelta callbacks, returns unsubscribe function
- `sendCommand()`: Send q:command, wait for q:command_ack (with 30s timeout)
- `onEvent()`: Register callback for q:event broadcasts (e.g., tool:request)
- Re-establishes all subscriptions on reconnect
- Handles ping/pong keep-alive

**src/shared/hooks/useWsToolRequest.ts**:
- Hook that listens for q:event "tool:request" and dispatches to handler callback
- Handler decides which methods to claim (match on method name)
- Sends response via q:tool_response (requestId + result or error)
- Module-level Set deduplicates responses across multiple hook instances
- Used by browser, simulator, and workspace RPC handlers

**src/shared/api/queryKeys.ts**:
- React Query key factory for workspaces, sessions, messages, stats
- Used by useQuerySubscription hook to auto-cache results

**src/shared/hooks/useQuerySubscription.ts** (custom React Query hook):
- Subscribes to WS resource, pushes snapshots into React Query cache
- Auto-unsubscribe on unmount
- Merges deltas client-side using `mergeDelta` function

**Data Flow (Frontend)**:
```
Frontend → q:subscribe/q:command → Backend WS
Backend → q:snapshot/q:delta/q:command_ack/q:event → Frontend
Frontend tool handler → q:tool_response → Backend
Backend tool-relay → respondToAgent → Agent-Server
```

---

### **6. PR Tracking & Workspace State**

**PR-related data**:
- Stored in `workspaces` table: `pr_url TEXT`, `pr_number INTEGER` (nullable)
- Queried on-demand via `GET /workspaces/:id/pr-status`
- Not persisted directly; computed each time via `gh pr list --head <branch>`
- Workspace displays PR state indicator (open/merged/closed) in sidebar
- CI status (passing/failing/pending) shown from statusCheckRollup
- Merge status (ready/blocked) from mergeable field
- Fork detection: upstream vs origin remote comparison

**Workspace state transitions**:
- initializing → ready (via workspace creation)
- ready → archived (via archive action)
- Sessions track: idle/working/error (persisted in sessions.status)

---

### **7. Current Architecture Summary**

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Frontend (React + Zustand + React Query)                        │
│ - useQuerySubscription: WS push → React Query cache             │
│ - useWsToolRequest: Listen for tool:request → respond           │
│ - WorkspaceItem: Displays pr_url, pr_number, session_status     │
└────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────┘
                             │ WebSocket q:* protocol
                             ↓
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Backend (Hono + Node.js)                                        │
│ - query-engine.ts: Routes q:subscribe/q:command                 │
│ - agent/service.ts: Manages AgentClient → sidecar               │
│ - agent/event-handler.ts: Canonical events → DB + invalidate    │
│ - agent/persistence.ts: DB writes for events                    │
│ - agent/tool-relay.ts: Tool request ↔ frontend                  │
│ - gh.service.ts: Queries GitHub via gh CLI                      │
│ - Database: SQLite (repositories, workspaces, sessions, msgs)   │
└────────────┬──────────────────────────────────┬──────────────────┘
             │ JSON-RPC 2.0                     │ REST
             ↓                                  ↓
   ┌─────────────────────┐          ┌──────────────────┐
   │ Agent-Server        │          │ Tauri IPC        │
   │ (sidecar, stateless)│          │ Git operations   │
   │ - Claude/Codex SDK  │          │ File scanning    │
   │ - EventBroadcaster  │          │ Terminal I/O     │
   │ - Health endpoints  │          └──────────────────┘
   └────────┬────────────┘
            ↓
   ┌─────────────────────┐
   │ Agent SDKs          │
   │ - Claude API        │
   │ - Tool execution    │
   └─────────────────────┘
```

---

### **8. Key Files & Their Purposes**

| File | Lines | Purpose |
|------|-------|---------|
| `backend/src/services/agent/client.ts` | 454 | JSON-RPC 2.0 WebSocket client → agent-server |
| `backend/src/services/agent/event-handler.ts` | 162 | Dispatch canonical events → persistence + invalidate |
| `backend/src/services/agent/persistence.ts` | 248 | DB writes for all event types |
| `backend/src/services/agent/tool-relay.ts` | 132 | Bidirectional tool request relay |
| `backend/src/services/query-engine.ts` | ~400 | q:* protocol frame dispatch |
| `sidecar/index.ts` | 653 | Agent-server entry point, transport agnostic |
| `sidecar/event-broadcaster.ts` | 619 | Multi-tunnel notification broadcaster |
| `shared/agent-events.ts` | 401 | Canonical event type definitions |
| `shared/schema.ts` | 143 | Database schema (5 tables, 10 indexes) |
| `src/platform/ws/query-protocol-client.ts` | ~400 | Frontend WS client singleton |
| `src/shared/hooks/useWsToolRequest.ts` | 100 | Frontend tool request listener hook |

---

### **9. Critical Data Flows**

**User sends message**:
1. Frontend: q:command { command: "sendMessage", sessionId, content, model }
2. Backend commands.ts: handleSendMessage() → writeUserMessage() → invalidate() → agent.forwardTurn()
3. Agent-server: agent.query() spawns Claude SDK stream
4. Agent-server: Emits canonical events (message.assistant, session.idle, etc.)
5. Backend event-handler: Listens on AgentClient, persists to DB, calls invalidate()
6. Query-engine: Pushes q:delta/q:snapshot to WS subscribers

**Tool request (browser, simulator, diff)**:
1. Agent-server calls EventBroadcaster.requestBrowserXxx()
2. Sidecar emits tool.request event via JSON-RPC to backend
3. Backend event-handler calls relay() → broadcasts q:event "tool:request"
4. Frontend useWsToolRequest hook catches event, dispatches to handler
5. Handler responds via q:tool_response
6. Backend tool-relay.resolve() resolves pending promise
7. event-handler sends respondToAgent() back to agent-server

**PR status query**:
1. Frontend requests GET /workspaces/:id/pr-status
2. Backend gh.service resolves HEAD branch, queries gh CLI
3. Returns pr_number, pr_url, pr_state, ci_status, review_status, merge_status
4. Workspace sidebar displays PR indicator

---

### **10. No Direct DB Access in Sidecar**

The agent-server is completely stateless:
- No database connection
- No persistence layer
- Events stream to backend via JSON-RPC notifications
- Backend owns all DB writes and invalidation logic
- Sidecar can be restarted without data loss

This design keeps the sidecar lightweight and fast, allowing rapid agent SDK upgrades without schema migrations.

---

This completes the comprehensive architecture analysis. The system is now fully decoupled with clear ownership: Backend handles persistence & subscriptions, Sidecar handles agent SDKs & tools, Frontend handles UI & WS protocol.

content.content:
1→/**
     2→ * OpenDevs Database Schema — Single source of truth.
     3→ *
     4→ * Imported by backend/src/lib/schema.ts.
     5→ * All statements are idempotent (IF NOT EXISTS).
     6→ *
     7→ * Tables: repositories, workspaces, sessions, messages, paired_devices
     8→ * Indexes: 10
     9→ * Triggers: 5 (3 auto-update updated_at, 2 denormalized message_count + auto-seq)
    10→ */
    11→
    12→/**
    13→ * Post-launch migrations.
    14→ * Each statement is an ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN that may already exist.
    15→ * Run each individually — catch "duplicate column" errors and skip.
    16→ * Runs AFTER SCHEMA_SQL so tables already exist.
    17→ *
    18→ * Currently empty — SCHEMA_SQL defines the full schema for fresh installs.
    19→ * Add ALTER TABLE statements here when the schema changes post-launch.
    20→ */
    21→export const MIGRATIONS: string[] = [
    22→  // sessions: structured error category for category-aware UI
    23→  `ALTER TABLE sessions ADD COLUMN error_category TEXT`,
    24→];
    25→
    26→export const SCHEMA_SQL = `
    27→  -- Repositories tracked by the app (id = UUID7, embeds created_at)
    28→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS repositories (
    29→    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,
    30→    name TEXT NOT NULL,
    31→    root_path TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    32→    git_default_branch TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'main',
    33→    sort_order INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    34→    git_origin_url TEXT,
    35→    updated_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
    36→  );
    37→
    38→  -- Git worktrees tied to repositories (id = UUID7, embeds created_at)
    39→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS workspaces (
    40→    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,
    41→    repository_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES repositories(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    42→    slug TEXT NOT NULL,
    43→    title TEXT,
    44→    git_branch TEXT,
    45→    git_target_branch TEXT,
    46→    state TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'initializing',
    47→    current_session_id TEXT,
    48→    pr_url TEXT,
    49→    pr_number INTEGER,
    50→    archive_commit TEXT,
    51→    archived_at TEXT,
    52→    setup_status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'none',
    53→    init_stage TEXT,
    54→    error_message TEXT,
    55→    updated_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
    56→  );
    57→
    58→  -- Agent sessions tied to workspaces (id = UUID7, embeds created_at)
    59→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sessions (
    60→    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,
    61→    workspace_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    62→    agent_type TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'claude',
    63→    model TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'opus',
    64→    agent_session_id TEXT,
    65→    title TEXT,
    66→    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'idle',
    67→    message_count INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    68→    error_message TEXT,
    69→    error_category TEXT,
    70→    last_user_message_at TEXT,
    71→    context_token_count INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    72→    context_used_percent REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    73→    is_hidden INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    74→    updated_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
    75→  );
    76→
    77→  -- Chat messages within sessions (id = UUID7, embeds created_at; append-only, no updated_at)
    78→  -- seq is a per-session monotonic integer for reliable cursor pagination.
    79→  -- Auto-assigned by trigger — never set manually in INSERT.
    80→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS messages (
    81→    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,
    82→    session_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    83→    seq INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    84→    role TEXT NOT NULL,
    85→    content TEXT,
    86→    turn_id TEXT,
    87→    model TEXT,
    88→    agent_message_id TEXT,
    89→    sent_at TEXT,
    90→    cancelled_at TEXT,
    91→    parent_tool_use_id TEXT
    92→  );
    93→
    94→  -- Paired devices for remote access authentication
    95→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS paired_devices (
    96→    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,
    97→    name TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'Unknown Device',
    98→    token_hash TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    99→    ip_address TEXT,
   100→    user_agent TEXT,
   101→    last_seen_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
   102→    created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
   103→  );
   104→
   105→  -- Indexes (10)
   106→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_workspaces_repository_id ON workspaces(repository_id);
   107→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_workspaces_state ON workspaces(state);
   108→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_sessions_workspace_id ON sessions(workspace_id);
   109→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_sessions_status ON sessions(status);
   110→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_messages_seq ON messages(session_id, seq DESC);
   111→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_messages_sent_at ON messages(session_id, sent_at);
   112→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_messages_session_role ON messages(session_id, role, id DESC);
   113→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_messages_turn_id ON messages(session_id, turn_id);
   114→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_messages_parent_tool_use ON messages(parent_tool_use_id);
   115→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_paired_devices_token_hash ON paired_devices(token_hash);
   116→
   117→  -- Triggers: auto-update updated_at (3)
   118→  CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS update_repositories_updated_at
   119→    AFTER UPDATE ON repositories

/**
 * OpenDevs Database Schema — Single source of truth.
 *
 * Imported by backend/src/lib/schema.ts.
 * All statements are idempotent (IF NOT EXISTS).
 *
 * Tables: repositories, workspaces, sessions, messages, paired_devices
 * Indexes: 10
 * Triggers: 5 (3 auto-update updated_at, 2 denormalized message_count + auto-seq)
 */

/**
 * Post-launch migrations.
 * Each statement is an ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN that may already exist.
 * Run each individually — catch "duplicate column" errors and skip.
 * Runs AFTER SCHEMA_SQL so tables already exist.
 *
 * Currently empty — SCHEMA_SQL defines the full schema for fresh installs.
 * Add ALTER TABLE statements here when the schema changes post-launch.
 */
export const MIGRATIONS: string[] = [
  // sessions: structured error category for category-aware UI
  `ALTER TABLE sessions ADD COLUMN error_category TEXT`,
];

export const SCHEMA_SQL = `
  -- Repositories tracked by the app (id = UUID7, embeds created_at)
  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS repositories (
    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,
    name TEXT NOT NULL,
    root_path TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    git_default_branch TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'main',
    sort_order INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    git_origin_url TEXT,
    updated_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
  );

  -- Git worktrees tied to repositories (id = UUID7, embeds created_at)
  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS workspaces (
    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,
    repository_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES repositories(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    slug TEXT NOT NULL,
    title TEXT,
    git_branch TEXT,
    git_target_branch TEXT,
    state TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'initializing',
    current_session_id TEXT,
    pr_url TEXT,
    pr_number INTEGER,
    archive_commit TEXT,
    archived_at TEXT,
    setup_status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'none',
    init_stage TEXT,
    error_message TEXT,
    updated_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
  );

  -- Agent sessions tied to workspaces (id = UUID7, embeds created_at)
  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sessions (
    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,
    workspace_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    agent_type TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'claude',
    model TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'opus',
    agent_session_id TEXT,
    title TEXT,
    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'idle',
    message_count INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    error_message TEXT,
    error_category TEXT,
    last_user_message_at TEXT,
    context_token_count INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    context_used_percent REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    is_hidden INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    updated_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
  );

  -- Chat messages within sessions (id = UUID7, embeds created_at; append-only, no updated_at)
  -- seq is a per-session monotonic integer for reliable cursor pagination.
  -- Auto-assigned by trigger — never set manually in INSERT.
  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS messages (
    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,
    session_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    seq INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    role TEXT NOT NULL,
    content TEXT,
    turn_id TEXT,
    model TEXT,
    agent_message_id TEXT,
    sent_at TEXT,
    cancelled_at TEXT,
    parent_tool_use_id TEXT
  );

  -- Paired devices for remote access authentication
  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS paired_devices (
    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,
    name TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'Unknown Device',
    token_hash TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    ip_address TEXT,
    user_agent TEXT,
    last_seen_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
  );

  -- Indexes (10)
  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_workspaces_repository_id ON workspaces(repository_id);
  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_workspaces_state ON workspaces(state);
  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_sessions_workspace_id ON sessions(workspace_id);
  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_sessions_status ON sessions(status);
  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_messages_seq ON messages(session_id, seq DESC);
  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_messages_sent_at ON messages(session_id, sent_at);
  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_messages_session_role ON messages(session_id, role, id DESC);
  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_messages_turn_id ON messages(session_id, turn_id);
  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_messages_parent_tool_use ON messages(parent_tool_use_id);
  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_paired_devices_token_hash ON paired_devices(token_hash);

  -- Triggers: auto-update updated_at (3)
  CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS update_repositories_updated_at
    AFTER UPDATE ON repositories
    BEGIN UPDATE repositories SET updated_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = NEW.id; END;

  CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS update_workspaces_updated_at
    AFTER UPDATE ON workspaces
    BEGIN UPDATE workspaces SET updated_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = NEW.id; END;

  CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS update_sessions_updated_at
    AFTER UPDATE ON sessions
    BEGIN UPDATE sessions SET updated_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = NEW.id; END;

  -- Triggers: denormalized message_count + auto-seq on messages (2)
  CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS assign_message_seq
    AFTER INSERT ON messages
    BEGIN
      UPDATE messages
        SET seq = (SELECT COALESCE(MAX(m.seq), 0) + 1 FROM messages m WHERE m.session_id = NEW.session_id AND m.id != NEW.id)
        WHERE id = NEW.id;
      UPDATE sessions SET message_count = message_count + 1 WHERE id = NEW.session_id;
    END;

  CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS dec_session_message_count
    AFTER DELETE ON messages
    BEGIN UPDATE sessions SET message_count = message_count - 1 WHERE id = OLD.session_id; END;
`;

1→/**
     2→ * Typed query functions — centralized DB reads for all route handlers.
     3→ *
     4→ * Every `as` cast lives here (once per query), not in routes.
     5→ * Route handlers call these functions instead of inline SQL.
     6→ */
     7→import type Database from 'better-sqlite3';
     8→import type {
     9→  RepositoryRow,
    10→  RepositoryWithCountsRow,
    11→  WorkspaceRow,
    12→  WorkspaceWithDetailsRow,
    13→  SessionRow,
    14→  SessionWithDetailsRow,
    15→  MessageRow,
    16→  StatsRow,
    17→} from './types';
    18→
    19→// ─── Workspace Queries ───────────────────────────────────────
    20→
    21→/**
    22→ * Canonical workspace + repo + session JOIN.
    23→ * Single source of truth — reused by list, get-by-id, and by-repo queries.
    24→ */
    25→const WORKSPACE_DETAILS_SELECT = `
    26→  SELECT
    27→    w.id, w.repository_id, w.slug, w.title, w.git_branch,
    28→    w.git_target_branch, w.state, w.current_session_id,
    29→    w.pr_url, w.pr_number,
    30→    w.setup_status, w.error_message, w.init_stage,
    31→    w.updated_at,
    32→    r.name as repo_name, r.root_path, r.git_default_branch,
    33→    s.status as session_status, s.model,
    34→    s.error_category as session_error_category,
    35→    s.error_message as session_error_message,
    36→    s.last_user_message_at as latest_message_sent_at
    37→  FROM workspaces w
    38→  LEFT JOIN repositories r ON w.repository_id = r.id
    39→  LEFT JOIN sessions s ON w.current_session_id = s.id
    40→`;
    41→
    42→export function getAllWorkspaces(db: Database.Database): WorkspaceWithDetailsRow[] {
    43→  return db.prepare(`
    44→    ${WORKSPACE_DETAILS_SELECT}
    45→    ORDER BY w.updated_at DESC
    46→    LIMIT 100
    47→  `).all() as WorkspaceWithDetailsRow[];
    48→}
    49→
    50→export function getWorkspacesByRepo(
    51→  db: Database.Database,
    52→  state?: string
    53→): WorkspaceWithDetailsRow[] {
    54→  // Support comma-separated states (e.g. "ready,initializing")
    55→  let stateFilter = '';
    56→  let stateParams: string[] = [];
    57→  if (state) {
    58→    const states = state.split(',').map(s => s.trim());
    59→    stateFilter = `WHERE w.state IN (${states.map(() => '?').join(',')})`;
    60→    stateParams = states;
    61→  }
    62→  return db.prepare(`
    63→    SELECT
    64→      w.id, w.repository_id, w.slug, w.title, w.git_branch,
    65→      w.git_target_branch, w.state, w.current_session_id,
    66→      w.pr_url, w.pr_number,
    67→      w.setup_status, w.error_message, w.init_stage,
    68→      w.updated_at,
    69→      r.name as repo_name, r.sort_order as repo_sort_order, r.root_path,
    70→      r.git_default_branch,
    71→      s.status as session_status, s.model,
    72→      s.error_category as session_error_category,
    73→      s.error_message as session_error_message,
    74→      s.last_user_message_at as latest_message_sent_at
    75→    FROM workspaces w
    76→    LEFT JOIN repositories r ON w.repository_id = r.id
    77→    LEFT JOIN sessions s ON w.current_session_id = s.id
    78→    ${stateFilter}
    79→    ORDER BY r.sort_order, r.name, w.updated_at DESC
    80→  `).all(...stateParams) as WorkspaceWithDetailsRow[];

lines: +# Workspace Status — Feature Specification & Implementation Guide
-Rebuilt for the post-refactor architecture where the sidecar is stateless and the backend agent service owns all persistence + invalidation.

+> **Context**: This document describes a new `status` column for workspaces that tracks workflow phase (backlog, in-progress, in-review, done, canceled). It was designed on the `seville-v2` branch which uses Tauri + React + Node.js backend + stateless agent-server sidecar. If the app has migrated to Electron, the Tauri-specific parts (IPC, Rust commands) won't apply, but the **data model, backend logic, protocol, and frontend state management are fully portable** since they live in TypeScript (shared/, backend/, src/).
-               └─ git worktree lifecycle — is the workspace usable?
-               └─ workflow phase — what stage is the work in? (NEW)
-               └─ agent activity — is the agent busy right now?
+Both are about *what's happening right now*. Neither answers the question a user actually cares about: **"What stage is this work in?"**
-All three are orthogonal. A workspace can be `state=ready`, `status=in-review`, `session_status=working` (agent doing follow-up on an open PR).
+A user managing 20+ parallel workspaces needs to see at a glance:
+- Which workspaces are parked for later (backlog)
+- Which are actively being worked on (in-progress)
+- Which have PRs out for review (in-review)

+This is the same mental model as Linear issue states. OpenDevs (the desktop app we run inside) already shipped this as `derived_status` + `manual_status` — two columns. We're doing it with **one column** and smarter rules.
-| `backlog` | **Sticky** | Only user can move it out |
-| `in-progress` | **Flow** | Default. Auto-progresses to `in-review` when PR is created |
-| `in-review` | **Flow** | Auto-set when `pr_url` is set. Auto-progresses to `done` on PR merge |
-| `done` | **Terminal** | Auto-set on PR merge or archive. Even overrides sticky states |
-| `canceled` | **Sticky** | Only user can move it out |

+OpenDevs uses `manual_status` (user override, nullable) + `derived_status` (auto-computed). The effective status is `manual_status ?? derived_status`. The problem: once the user overrides, the system doesn't know when to resume auto-deriving. The two realities diverge silently.
-**Sticky rule**: `backlog` and `canceled` resist auto-derive events. Only explicit user action (or archive, which is definitive) can move them.

+Our approach: **single `status` column** with **sticky vs flow** semantics. Some states resist auto-updates (sticky), others welcome auto-progression (flow). No ghost state, no ambiguity about which column to read.
-| Trigger | Where in code | Status transition |
-| PR URL set | `PATCH /workspaces/:id` when `pr_url` changes | `in-progress` → `in-review` (if flow state) |
-| Workspace archived | `archiveWorkspace` mutation / `PATCH /workspaces/:id` | any → `done` (forced, even sticky) |
-| User manual set | `updateWorkspaceStatus` mutation | any → any (always allowed) |
-| Future: PR merged | GitHub webhook or polling | `in-review` → `done` (if flow state) |
-### Where auto-derive hooks into the new architecture

-**Auto-derive is a pure backend concern.** It's a function called as a side-effect of existing operations — not a new event type, not a sidecar concern. The sidecar doesn't know about PRs or workflow status.
+| `backlog` | **Sticky** | Parked for later | Only user can move it out |
+| `in-progress` | **Flow** | Active work (default) | Auto-progresses to `in-review` when PR created |
+| `in-review` | **Flow** | PR is open | Auto-progresses to `done` when PR merged |
+| `done` | **Terminal** | Work is complete | Auto-set on PR merge or archive |
+| `canceled` | **Sticky** | Abandoned | Only user can move it out |
+**Sticky states** (`backlog`, `canceled`) resist system auto-progression. The user intentionally parked the workspace. Only explicit user action (or archiving, which is definitive) can change them.
+**Flow states** (`in-progress`, `in-review`) welcome auto-progression. When a signal arrives (PR created, PR merged), the system moves the workspace forward.
+**Terminal state** (`done`) is the end of the flow. Can be set by system (PR merge, archive) or user.
- * Respects sticky states unless forced (archive is definitive).
- * Won't regress (in-review → in-progress is a no-op).
-export function autoProgressStatus(workspaceId: string, target: WorkspaceStatus, opts?: { force?: boolean }): void
+**Archive override**: Archiving a workspace *always* forces `done`, even from sticky states. Archive is a definitive lifecycle event.
- * Explicitly set workspace status (user override). No sticky/flow guards.
-export function setWorkspaceStatus(workspaceId: string, status: WorkspaceStatus): void
-### Call sites (4 total, all in backend)
+               └─ What stage is the work in? (workflow) — NEW
+               └─ Is the agent busy right now? (activity, per-session)
+All three are **orthogonal**. A workspace can be:
+- `state=ready` + `status=in-review` + `session_status=working` → agent doing follow-up on an open PR
-4. **Future**: PR merge detection (GitHub webhook or session-end check).

+Currently `pr_url` and `pr_number` columns exist in the schema but are **never written** — PR status is fetched on-demand from `gh CLI`. For auto-derive to work, we need to persist `pr_url`/`pr_number` when the frontend fetches PR status and a PR is found. This gives us a reliable DB signal for `in-progress → in-review` transitions.
-### NOT a call site: agent event handler

+**Important**: We should NOT make `pr_url` the single source of truth for "has a PR". The `gh CLI` on-demand check remains the authoritative source for detailed PR state (CI, reviews, conflicts). The persisted `pr_url` is just a flag for auto-derive — "at some point, a PR was created for this workspace."

-The agent event handler (`event-handler.ts`) handles session lifecycle, not workspace workflow. Session events (`session.started`, `session.idle`, etc.) don't directly trigger status changes — a workspace can have multiple sessions, the agent starting/stopping doesn't change what workflow phase the work is in.
+| # | Trigger Event | Where It Fires | Condition | Status Transition |
+| 2 | PR detected | When `pr_url` is persisted (see §4.5) | Status is flow state AND not already `in-review`/`done` | `in-progress` → `in-review` |
+| 3 | PR merged | When PR status returns `merged` (see §4.5) | Status is flow state | `in-review` → `done` |
+| 4 | Workspace archived | `archiveWorkspace` mutation or `PATCH` | **Always (forced)** — archive is definitive | any → `done` |
+| 5 | Workspace unarchived | `PATCH /workspaces/:id` with `state=ready` | Status is `done` (set by archive) | `done` → `in-progress` |
+| 6 | User manual set | `updateWorkspaceStatus` mutation | **Always** — user intent overrides all | any → any |

+- **Agent session events** (session.started, session.idle, session.error): These are per-session activity signals. A workspace can have multiple sessions. The agent starting/stopping doesn't change the workflow phase.
+- **Message events**: New messages don't affect workflow status.
+- **Setup status changes**: Dependency install completing is a lifecycle concern, not workflow.

+/** Sticky states resist auto-progression. Only user action (or archive) can exit. */
export const STICKY_STATUSES: ReadonlySet<WorkspaceStatus> = new Set(["backlog", "canceled"]);

+4. **No new Tauri/Electron events** — Data flows through the WS protocol.
+5. **No sidecar changes** — The sidecar is stateless and doesn't know about workflow status.
+6. **No separate status polling** — PR status is already fetched on-demand; we just add a persist side-effect.
+7. **No GitHub webhooks** — Auto-derive is triggered by the existing PR status fetch path.
-| 3 | `shared/schema.ts` | Add column to CREATE TABLE + index + migration entry | +4 |
-| 6 | `backend/src/db/queries.ts` | Add `w.status` to SELECT + stats counts + backfill fn | +15 |
-| 10 | `backend/src/routes/workspaces.ts` | Hook auto-derive into PATCH (archive, unarchive, PR) | +15 |
+For context, here's how OpenDevs (the production app we run inside) implements this:
-**Total: ~160 lines across 14 files (1 new file, 13 edits)**
+- `archive_commit TEXT` — hash of last commit when archived
+- Two columns creates ghost state (derived keeps computing while ignored)
+- No clear rule for when to clear `manual_status` after override
+- Our sticky/flow model is simpler and handles the same cases
+If the app has migrated from Tauri to Electron:
+- `shared/` — All types, enums, schema, events (pure TypeScript)
+- `backend/` — All routes, services, DB queries, query engine (Node.js)
+- `src-tauri/` → Not applicable in Electron. Git operations and file scanning that were in Rust would need equivalent implementations.
+- Process lifecycle — How the backend and sidecar are started/managed would differ.
+- Database path — `~/Library/Application Support/com.opendevs.app/opendevs.db` may be different.
+**The workspace status feature does not touch any Tauri-specific code.** All changes are in shared/, backend/, and src/ — fully portable.
+| 3 | `shared/schema.ts` | Edit | +4 | Add column to CREATE TABLE + index + migration entry |
+| 6 | `backend/src/db/queries.ts` | Edit | +20 | Add `w.status` to all SELECTs + stats counts + backfill function |
+| 10 | `backend/src/routes/workspaces.ts` | Edit | +15 | Hook auto-derive into PATCH (archive, unarchive, manual) |
+| 11 | `backend/src/routes/workspaces.pr.ts` | Edit | +12 | Persist `pr_url` + trigger auto-derive on PR discovery/merge |
+- Test setWorkspaceStatus always writes regardless of current state
+- Manual override from any state to any state
+- Status icon renders for each status value

content.text:
Done. The spec is at `.context/workspace-status-plan.md` and pushed to `zvadaadam/explore-workspace-states`.

**Key highlights for developers on the new branch:**

- **~180 lines across 15 files** (1 new file, 14 edits) — deliberately small scope
- **All changes are in `shared/`, `backend/`, and `src/`** — zero Tauri/Rust code, fully portable to Electron
- **Single `status` column** with sticky/flow rules, not OpenDevs's two-column approach
- **Auto-derive hooks into existing paths** — PR status fetch, archive mutation, PATCH route. No new polling, no webhooks
- **`pr_url` needs to be persisted** — columns exist but are never written today. The PR status endpoint is the natural place to persist it as a side-effect
- **Sidecar is untouched** — it's stateless, doesn't know about workflow status
